title: add CONTAINERS_CONF_OVERRIDE
body:
Add yet another environment variable for loading containers.conf. When CONTAINERS_CONF_OVERRIDE is set, the specified config file will be loaded last - even when CONTAINERS_CONF is set.

This mechanism is needed to preserve system settings and other environment variables.  Setting CONTAINERS_CONF will load only the specified config file and ignore all system and user paths. That makes testing hard as many Podman tests use CONTAINERS_CONF for testing.

The intended use of CONTAINERS_CONF_OVERRIDE is to set it during tests and point it to a specific configuration of Podman (e.g., netavark with sqlite backend).

Similar needs have popped up talking to users in the automotive and high-performance computing space.  In a way, such a setting allows for specifying a specific "flavor" of Podman while preserving all existing settings on the system.

something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

> Currently vendoring my fork from [containers/common#1378](https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1378).

Please comment in https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1378
int tests break because of the SYS_CHROOT default cap change.  Will update it accordingly once https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1378 is in.
Oh, also, every instance of `CONTAINERS_CONF` under `tests/` will need to be evaluated to see if it should stay that way or use the new `OVERRIDE`. But that's an exercise for later.

> Oh, also, every instance of `CONTAINERS_CONF` under `tests/` will need to be evaluated to see if it should stay that way or use the new `OVERRIDE`. But that's an exercise for later.

Ideally, no test should ever use _OVERRIDE.  It should only be set by CI/users to specify the "flavor" of Podman (e.g., netavark + sqlite).  Tests can continue to use _CONF but I totally agree that we need some auditing to make sure it will work as intended.
Oh yikes. We have a serious, serious disconnect here. Will resolve later today, after all the meetings.

> Oh yikes. We have a serious, serious disconnect here. Will resolve later today, after all the meetings.

Let's put our heads together early next week!

Look at all the `CONTAINERS_CONF=xxx` instances under ~~test/e2e~~ `test/system`. I haven't looked at each individual one, but the overall pattern is: those do totally the wrong thing when `/etc/containers/containers.conf` sets `runc`, or `sqlite`, or whatever comes next. Almost all of those must use `OVERRIDE`. I haven't evaluated `tests/e2e`, maybe that's different.

[EDIT: fix a mistype]

> Look at all the `CONTAINERS_CONF=xxx` instances under ~test/e2e~ `test/system`. I haven't looked at each individual one, but the overall pattern is: those do totally the wrong thing when `/etc/containers/containers.conf` sets `runc`, or `sqlite`, or whatever comes next. Almost all of those must use `OVERRIDE`. I haven't evaluated `tests/e2e`, maybe that's different.

What I have in mind is to entirely ignore `/etc/containers` or `~/.config/containers` etc.  for the tests.  It may work to set these configs in CI VMs but it's impractical on developer machines to change the system like that.

Instead, I want to use `CONTAINERS_CONF_OVERRIDE=./test/system/sqlite_runc.conf`.  This way, the _OVERRIDE conf make sure that all tests are _forced_ to use the settings in the specific file.

Hope this make sense.

It makes sense but it's a complete nonstarter. System tests, by design, *trust their environment*. Whatever is set up in `/etc/containers/whatever.conf`, that is what they test. That is what they must test. (As opposed to e2e tests, which try to ignore their environment, by specifying a long long list of `--this-and-that` options. This is never going to happen for system tests).

In a system test, the use of `CONTAINERS_CONF` negates that. That is the whole point behind #15413 and why it's so imperative to resolve that. See that issue for further discussion.

If we don't have a `--dump-config` option, then I will need to switch system tests to use `CONTAINERS_CONF_OVERRIDE`. Either that, or formalize my kludgy `toml-edit` approach from #17831.
Thanks, Ed!  I now understand where you're going. If that is the goal, I agree that most tests need to migrate over to use _OVERRIDE.
